_description: WSET-AI-System Trust Tier Matrix. Defines the complete data governance rules for each source type in the system. For each source, specifies: trust tier, access permissions per agent, retrieval weight, calibration status, whether it is graph-eligible, and specific agent access rules. This matrix is the single authoritative reference for source-level governance decisions. Cross-reference with retrieval_priority_matrix.json for retrieval weights and AGENT_BOUNDARIES.md for the full governance framework.

tier_0.name: Official Marking Authority
tier_0.description: WSET-issued documents with direct scoring authority: mark schemes, examiner guidance, grade descriptors.

tier_1.name: Official WSET Specification
tier_1.description: WSET-published course materials, textbooks, unit specifications.

tier_2.name: Official SAT Framework
tier_2.description: WSET Systematic Approach to Tasting official documentation.

tier_3.name: Validated Benchmark Answers
tier_3.description: Human-reviewed exemplar answers calibrated to P/M/D standards.

tier_4.name: Pedagogical Enrichment
tier_4.description: High-quality educational content not officially WSET-sanctioned: Wine With Jimmy, Diploma enrichment, knowledge graph nodes.

tier_5.name: Generated / Internal
tier_5.description: AI-generated content, draft materials, internal examples pending review.

tutor_agent.notes: Tutor Agent uses WSET official materials as the factual and structural anchor. May not reproduce mark scheme language verbatim. May paraphrase exam requirements and grade criteria.

examiner_agent.notes: Examiner Agent's primary and mandatory source. All scoring decisions must trace to this source tier.

tutor_agent.notes: The SAT is the mandatory vocabulary standard for all tasting notes. Tutor Agent must use SAT terminology for any sensory description guidance.

examiner_agent.notes: SAT carries equivalent scoring authority to Tier 0 for tasting-based assessment questions.

tutor_agent.notes: Tutor Agent reference use only. Must not expose mark scheme language directly to learners during assessment simulation. May use post-assessment to explain what was required.

examiner_agent.notes: Primary scoring authority. Session-specific; expires after the exam session to which it applies.

tutor_agent.notes: Validated benchmark answers are high-value for showing learners what Distinction looks like. Must carry individual L4 human sign-off.

examiner_agent.notes: May inform calibration but never determines a score. Maximum weight 0.70 for Examiner Agent.

tutor_agent.notes: Used for practice questions, spaced repetition, and weakness-targeted retrieval. Items with needs_human_review=true must be flagged to learners as unvalidated. allowed_for_examiner=false for all items.

examiner_agent.notes: The internal question bank has source_trust_tier=internal_training and allowed_for_examiner=False for all records. It must never enter the Examiner Agent corpus.

tutor_agent.framing_statement: This comes from an informal pedagogical source and is for enrichment only — it is not WSET official guidance.
tutor_agent.notes: Requires human review and WSET accuracy check before ingestion. Must never be used for exam strategy, distinction training, or any content that claims to represent WSET marking criteria.

examiner_agent.notes: Absolutely and unconditionally forbidden for Examiner Agent use. No exception case exists.

diploma_content.label: WSET Diploma (L4) Content as L3 Enrichment

tutor_agent.framing_statement: This is Diploma-level content used here for deeper understanding — it goes beyond what is required for the WSET Level 3 exam.
tutor_agent.notes: Must carry explicit L3-downgrade annotation. May not introduce Diploma marking criteria or Diploma-level expectations. Access restricted to advanced_enrichment and distinction_training roles only.

examiner_agent.notes: Different qualification level. May not be used to interpret or supplement L3 mark schemes. Forbidden unconditionally.

tutor_agent.framing_statement: This explanation was generated by an AI system. Please verify against your official WSET course materials.
tutor_agent.notes: Used only when no higher-tier source covers the specific query. Must be labelled as AI-generated. graph_eligible=false until human-promoted.

examiner_agent.notes: No traceability to WSET authority. Forbidden unconditionally.

tutor_agent.notes: Forced retrieval when detection_signal match occurs. Not scored — always included. High pedagogical value. Not framing-required because correction itself is the framing.

examiner_agent.notes: Examiner Agent does not perform misconception correction. Unconditionally forbidden.

tutor_agent.notes: Core Distinction-level content. Graph traversal bonus +0.10 applies. Not framing-required because causal chains represent pedagogically constructed (not informal or generated) knowledge.

examiner_agent.notes: Pedagogical depth content is not marking authority. Forbidden unconditionally.
